incident_reconstruction.reconstruction: The vehicle was involved in a head-on collision with another vehicle, likely traveling in the opposite direction. The front-end damage is consistent with a high-speed impact. The airbag likely deployed given the severity of the damage.
reconstruction_bullets: Severe damage to the front bumper, bonnet, and headlights indicates a high-energy impact.
The damage pattern suggests a direct frontal collision with another vehicle or object.
The described incident of a vehicle coming from the wrong side and hitting the claimant's vehicle matches the observed damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Pre-Incident
storyboard_panels.description: The Hyundai Venue SX was traveling on a road when it was struck by an oncoming vehicle.

storyboard_panels.title: Point of Impact
storyboard_panels.description: The oncoming vehicle hit the Hyundai Venue SX directly on the front end, causing severe damage.

storyboard_panels.title: Immediate Aftermath
storyboard_panels.description: The vehicle was severely damaged, with the front bumper, bonnet, and headlights destroyed. The airbag likely deployed.

storyboard_panels.title: Evidence Summary
storyboard_panels.description: The physical evidence supports the claimant's story of a head-on collision with a vehicle from the wrong side.

incident_reconstruction.summary: Most Probable: Front impact | Confidence: 85% | Story Match: Yes

decision_reasons: Fraud score of 42 exceeds the Approve threshold of 40, indicating a Medium risk
Claim filed 7 days after incident, which is an indicator of potential fraud (NEW POLICY SYNDROME — MEDIUM RISK)
Physics Mismatch [Damage Inconsistency] detected, which raises concerns about the claim's validity
Multiple vehicles visible in claim images, which may indicate a staged accident
reasoning_trail: Step 1: Damage Assessment indicates severe front-end damage consistent with a high-speed impact
Step 2: Fraud Intelligence reveals a Medium risk fraud score of 42 with indicators of new policy syndrome and physics mismatch
Step 3: Incident Reconstruction confirms the collision type as front impact with a confidence level of 85%
Step 4: Context & Policy verification confirms the location and policy coverage with no obvious exclusions
Step 5: Historical Precedents do not provide a direct match, but the fraud score and indicators warrant escalation
Step 6: Settlement Calculation is not applicable due to the escalation decision
Step 7: Final Decision is to escalate the claim for further investigation due to the Medium risk fraud score and inconsistent indicators
